title: City Council Regular Session Meeting 6/2/2026
agendaText: City Council Regular Session Meeting - June 02, 2026 Agenda Full Package Agenda Full Package Open Video in new Window Video City Council Agenda City of Campbell, 70 N. First St., Campbell, California NOTE: This City Council Regular meeting will be conducted in person as well as telecommunication and is compliant with provisions of the Brown Act. Vice Mayor Bybee will attend remotely from Hampton Inn Pendleton 101 S.W. Nye, Pendelton OR, 97801. A quorum of the City Council will be in attendance at the Campbell City Hall Council Chambers. The City Council meeting will be live-streamed on Channel 26 (Xfinity) or Channel 88 (Direct TV), the City's website and on YouTube https://www.youtube.com/user/CityofCampbell for those who only wish to view the meeting. Those members of the public wishing to provide public comment virtually are asked to register in advance at: https://www.campbellca.gov/signup . After registering, you will receive a confirmation email containing information about joining the meeting. Members of the public may attend the meeting in person at Campbell City Hall - Council Chambers. Public comment will also be accepted via email at [email protected] until 12:00 p.m. prior to the start of the meeting. Written comments will be posted on the website and distributed to the Council. If you choose to email your comments, please indicate in the subject line “FOR PUBLIC COMMENT” and indicate the item number. REGULAR MEETING OF THE CAMPBELL CITY COUNCIL Tuesday, June 02, 2026 07:00 PM City Hall Council Chamber 1. CALL TO ORDER/ROLL CALL 2. PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE 3. SPECIAL PRESENTATIONS AND PROCLAMATIONS Please Note: Items listed under Special Presentations and Proclamations are considered ceremonial. The City Council will not take action on these matters. 4. COMMUNICATIONS AND PETITIONS 5. ORAL REQUESTS NOTE: This portion of the meeting is reserved for persons wishing to address the City Council on any matter not on the agenda. Persons wishing to address the Council are requested, but not required to complete a Speaker’s Card. Speakers are limited to two (2) minutes. The law generally prohibits the Council from discussion or taking action on such items. However, the Council may instruct staff accordingly regarding Oral Requests. 6. COUNCIL ANNOUNCEMENTS 7. CONSENT CALENDAR NOTE: All matters listed under consent calendar are considered by the City Council to be routine and will be enacted by one motion. There will be no separate discussion of these items unless a request is made by a member of City Council, City staff, or a member of the public. Any person wishing to speak on any item on the consent calendar should ask to have the item removed from the consent calendar prior to the time the Council votes to approve. If removed, the item will be discussed in the order in which it appears. 7.1 Minutes of City Council Regular Meeting of May 19, 2026 Recommended Action Approve the regular meeting minutes of May 19, 2026. 7.2 Approving Payment of Bills and Claims 1. Report Approving Bills and Claims 06.02.26.pdf Recommended Action Approve the bills and claims in the amount of $1,865,798.68. 7.3 Monthly Investment Transactions Report- April 2026 1. Report Monthly Investment Transactions Report- April 2026.pdf 2. Monthly Investment tranx rpt- Apr'26.pdf 3. Chandler BNY Investment Statement- 4.30.26.pdf Recommended Action That the City Council review and accept the Monthly Investment Transactions Report for April 2026. 7.4 Monthly Treasurer’s Report (April 2026) 1. Report - Monthly Treasurer's Report April 2026.pdf 2. 2026.04 Fund Balance Report.pdf Recommended Action That the City Council review and accept the Monthly Treasurer’s Report for the month ended April 30, 2026. 7.5 Adoption of Ordinance 26-2348 renewing Ordinance 2280 to approve the Military Use Policy 1. Report - Ordinance Adoption of AB 481 Military Equipment Report.pdf 2. Ordinance 26-2348 - Military Equipment Use.pdf Recommended Action That the City Council take second reading to adopt Ordinance 26-2348. 7.6 Approval of Transportation Development Act (TDA) Article 3 Grant Application for Fiscal Year 2026/27 (Resolution/Roll Call Vote) 1. report-authorization to submit TDA grant application 6-2-26.pdf 2. Attachment 1 - Resolution-TDA Campbell Bicycle and Pedestrian Project FY 26-27 project 6-2-26 consent.pdf 3. Attachments A and B of Council Resolution for Campbell TDA Grant Application FY 2026-27.pdf 4. Attachment 2 - TDA grant project vicinity map - 6-2-26.pdf Recommended Action That the City Council adopt a resolution authorizing the Public Works Director to submit a grant application to the Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority (VTA) for $82,659 in Transportation Development Act (TDA) Article 3 Guarantee Funds for the Campbell Bicycle and Pedestrian Project Fiscal Year (FY) 26/27 . 7.7 Preliminary Approval of Engineer’s Report and Adoption of Resolution of Intention, Lighting and Landscaping District LLA-1, Fiscal Year (FY) 2026-27 and Set Time and Place for Public Hearing (Resolution/Roll Call Vote) 1. Report - Preliminary Approval of Engineer’s Report for LLA-1 for FY26-27.pdf 2. FY 26-27 LLA Resolution of Approval.pdf 3. FY 26-27 LLA Resolution of Intention.pdf Recommended Action That the City Council adopt a resolution preliminarily approving the Engineer’s Report for the annual levy and collection of assessments for City of Campbell Lighting District LLA-1 for Fiscal Year 2026-27; and adopt a resolution declaring Council’s intention to levy and collect assessments within the City of Campbell Lighting District LLA-1 for (FY) 2026-27 and setting forth the time and place for a required Public Hearing. 8. PUBLIC HEARINGS AND INTRODUCTIONS OF ORDINANCES NOTE: Members of the public may be allotted up to two (2) minutes to comment on any public hearing item. Applicants/Appellants and their representatives may be allotted up to a total of five (5) minutes for opening statements and up to a total of three (3) minutes maximum for closing statements. Items requested/recommended for continuance are subject to Council’s consent at the meeting. 8.1 Public Hearing to Consider Adoption of an Ordinance Extending Interim Ordinance No. 26-2347, which Continues Implementation of the Starter Home Revitalization Act with a Revised Method of Applying Vacancy Requirements to Single-Family Residential Properties (Ordinance/ 4/5 Roll Call Vote). 1. Report – Extension of Interim Ordinance No. 26-2347 Implementing the Starter Home Revitalization Act.pdf 2. Interim Ordinance Extension.pdf Recommended Action It is recommended that the City Council adopt an ordinance to extend Interim Ordinance No. 26-2347, for a period for 10 months and 15 days. 8.2 Public Hearing to consider the adoption of an Interim Ordinance, identifying specific sites for exclusion from Senate Bill 79 (SB 79) applicability, amending Section 21.02.020.E (Conflicting Requirements) of the Campbell Municipal Code to clarify the applicability of State and Federal preemptions to the Zoning Code, including as related to the implementation of Senate Bill 79, amending the Zoning Map to add a reference to Historic Structures of Merit, and authorizing submittal to the California Department of Housing and Community Development (HCD) for statutory review. (Ordinance/ Roll Call Vote) 1. SB 79 - Site Removals.pdf 2. Attachment 1 - CC Ord. - SB 79 Site Removals (final).pdf 3. Attachment 2 - SB 79 Study Area Changes (8.5x11).pdf 4. Attachment 3 - Updated Scenario 1 - No Alternative Plan (8.5x11).pdf Recommended Action That the City Council take the following action: Adopt an Interim Ordinance, identifying specific sites for exclusion from Senate Bill 79 (SB 79) applicability, amending Section 21.02.020.E (Conflicting Requirements) of the Campbell Municipal Code to clarify the applicability of State and Federal preemptions to the Zoning Code, including as related to the implementation of Senate Bill 79, amending the Zoning Map to add a reference to Historic Structures of Merit, and authorizing submittal to the California Department of Housing and Community Development (HCD) for statutory review. 8.3 Public Hearing to Consider Adoption of an Ordinance to Extend Interim Ordinance No. 2331 Which Established Urgency Measures in Response to the 2018 Small Cell Order (FCC 18-133) by the Federal Communications Commission (File No. PLN-2025-50) (Ordinance / Roll Call Vote) 1. Extending Wireless Interim Ordinance.pdf 2. Attachment 1 - Extending Ordinance.pdf 3. Attachment 2 - CC Ordinance 2331.pdf 4. Attachment 3 - CC Ordinance 2333.pdf 5. Attachment 4 - Public Comments.pdf Recommended Action That the City Council adopt an Ordinance, extending Interim Ordinance No. 2331, which established urgency measures in response to the 2018 Small Cell Order (FCC 18-133) by the Federal Communications Commission. 9. NEW BUSINESS 9.1 Approval and Award of a New Janitorial Service Contract for City Owned Buildings (City Hall, Lower Police Annex, Police Operations Building, Historical Fire House Museum, Ainsley House, Carriage House, 2nd Street Garage and the City Service Center) (Resolution/Roll Call Vote) 1. Report - Approval and Award of a New Janitorial Service Contract for City Owned Buildings.pdf 2. RESOLUTION for janitorial contract FY27 final.pdf 3. Signed contract by vendor 2026-05-11.pdf Recommended Action That the City Council adopt a resolution: 1) approving and awarding the contract for the janitorial services to the Impec Group, LLC in an amount not to exceed $264,445 for city owned buildings (City Hall, Lower Police Annex, Police Operations Building, Historical Fire House Museum, Ainsley House, Carriage House, 2nd Street Garage and the City Service Center); and 2) authorizing the City Manager to execute said contract as specified in Campbell Municipal Code Section 3.20.100 for a period of one year plus the option to include up to five (5) additional one (1) year extensions for a total of six (6) years. 9.2 Fiscal Year 2027 Budget and CIP Introduction 1. Report-Fiscal Year 2027 Budget and CIP Introduction.pdf 2. FY 2027 City Manager Transmittlal Letter (INTRO 06-02-26) FINAL.pdf 3. Revised Service Objectives (06-02-26).pdf Recommended Action That the City Council review the Proposed Fiscal Year (FY) 2027 Operating Budget and 2027 - 2031 Capital Improvement Plan (CIP) as presented; provide direction to staff to proceed with finalizing the budget; and establish June 16, 2026 as the date for a public hearing and adoption of the FY 2027 Operating Budget and CIP, as well as the Gann Appropriations Limit. 10. UNFINISHED BUSINESS 11. COUNCIL COMMITTEE REPORTS 11.1 Council Committee Reports 1. Report - Council Committee Reports 6.2.26.pdf Recommended Action That the City Council report on activities from their committee assignments. 12. ADJOURN IMPORTANT NOTICE: Materials related to an item on this agenda submitted to the City Council after distribution of the agenda packet are available for public inspection with the agenda packet in the lobby of City Clerk’s Office, 70 N. First Street, Campbell, CA 95008, during normal business hours. These materials will also be available on the City website at https://pub-campbell.escribemeetings.com with the agenda packet following the last item of the agenda, subject to staff’s ability to post the documents prior to the meeting. All documents not posted prior to the meeting will be posted the next business day. In compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act, listening assistive devices are available for all meetings held in the City Council Chambers. If you require accommodation, please contact the City Clerk’s Office, (408) 866-2117, at least one week in advance of the meeting. No Item Selected This item has no attachments 1. Extending Wireless Interim Ordinance.pdf 2. Attachment 1 - Extending Ordinance.pdf 3. Attachment 2 - CC Ordinance 2331.pdf 4. Attachment 3 - CC Ordinance 23
